subject: Sociology
title: The Racial Wealth Gap
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Racial Wealth Gap.

hook.text: The median white family holds eight times the wealth of the median Black family. That gap didn't happen by accident.

buildup.text: Slavery, Jim Crow, redlining, and discriminatory lending systematically blocked Black wealth accumulation for centuries.

discovery.text: The GI Bill gave white veterans homes and college while largely excluding Black veterans from the same benefits.

twist.text: Equal income doesn't produce equal wealth. A Black family earning $100k has far less net worth than a white one.

climax.text: The gap compounds generationally. Without inherited wealth, each generation starts over while others build on a base.

punchline.text: The gap wasn't created by choices. It was built by policy.

quiz.question: What contributed to the racial wealth gap?
options: Discriminatory policies like redlining and the GI Bill
Equal access to all government programs
Differences in personal savings habits only
